key_questions.question: Why is Paramount paying $31.00/share all-cash — roughly a 139% premium over WBD's undisturbed price of $12.54 (Sept 10, 2025) — and how much of that premium is contested-auction dynamics (Netflix and Comcast bidding) versus a unique strategic lever only Paramount can pull?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Separates 'won a bidding war / overpaid' from 'real synergy lever that justifies the price', which determines whether the premium is recoverable or value-destroying.

key_questions.question: Why did the WBD board flip from the Netflix cash-and-stock deal ($27.75/share, studios+streaming only with linear spun off) to Paramount's all-cash $31 whole-company offer — was the deciding factor price, cash certainty, or that Paramount absorbed the regulatory risk?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Reveals what the board actually weighted (headline value vs. certainty vs. risk transfer) and what WBD shareholders are really receiving versus the road not taken.

key_questions.question: Why fund a ~$111B all-cash deal with ~$50B+ of new debt (pro-forma ~6-7x EBITDA, ~$79-87B total debt) to buy a company whose largest segment — Global Linear Networks — is in structural decline, and how does the combined entity service that debt if linear erodes faster than planned?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Tests whether the pro-forma balance sheet survives a faster cord-cutting scenario; this is the central tail risk of the transaction.

key_questions.question: WBD was itself about to split into Warner Bros. (studios+streaming) and Discovery Global (networks); why is Paramount buying the WHOLE company including the declining linear networks rather than only the studio/streaming crown jewels — does it want the networks' cash flow to service debt, or is it stuck absorbing a melting asset to get HBO/Warner?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Clarifies whether Paramount is paying for an asset it values or tolerating a declining business as the price of acquiring the IP it actually wants.

key_questions.question: Paramount+ and HBO Max are each sub-scale against Netflix and Disney; what does combining them concretely change in streaming unit economics — content amortization spread over a larger subscriber base, reduced churn, pricing power — is there a real density lever, or just additive scale?
key_questions.why_it_matters: This is the core revenue-synergy thesis; per the synergy method a revenue synergy earns credit only with a named mechanism, not 'a bigger platform'.

key_questions.question: Why did the DOJ clear a combination of two major film/TV studios plus CBS News and CNN with NO divestitures and no conditions on June 12, 2026, against a historically tougher media-antitrust backdrop — what explains it (streaming-era market definition, or political alignment of the Ellison ownership with the administration)?
key_questions.why_it_matters: The clearance is the pivotal certainty event; understanding WHY it cleared tells us how durable that clearance is against the threatened state-AG challenge.

key_questions.question: What is the substance and likelihood of the threatened multi-state attorneys-general lawsuit — and the surrounding political controversy (FCC 'capitulation' dissent, Senate-Democrat foreign-influence concerns over Oracle/Ellison) — and can a state challenge actually block or materially delay a deal the federal DOJ has already cleared without conditions?
key_questions.why_it_matters: The principal residual close risk after DOJ clearance; sizes the gap between 'cleared' and 'closed' and the timeline to Q3 2026.

key_questions.question: Why did Paramount agree to seller-favorable risk allocation — a $7B reverse regulatory termination fee, paying WBD's $2.8B Netflix break fee, and a $0.25/quarter ticking fee for delays past Sept 30, 2026 — and what does that signal about Paramount's confidence in clearance versus its determination to win at any cost?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Deal-protection terms reveal each side's leverage and how hard the buyer will push to close — a key input to the close-probability force balance.

key_questions.question: What synergies has Paramount actually disclosed ($ amount, timing, cost vs. revenue), and what annual synergy level would be needed to offset the premium and the incremental interest burden — modest or heroic?
key_questions.why_it_matters: The break-even test: a deal needing modest synergies is lower-risk; one hinging on heroic, mostly-revenue synergies is an execution gamble.

key_questions.question: Why this buyer, why now: what can David Ellison's Paramount Skydance — formed only in August 2025 and backed by Ellison-family / Oracle wealth and RedBird capital — do with WBD that Netflix or Comcast could not, and why launch a $100B+ megadeal within months of its own formation?
key_questions.why_it_matters: The synthesis 'why': distinguishes a specific strategic logic (scale to challenge Netflix/Disney; consolidate the streaming endgame) from empire-building or a politically-enabled land grab.

planned_sources.title: Warner Bros. Discovery FY2025 Form 10-K (filed 2026)
planned_sources.rationale: Primary source for WBD segment revenue mix (Streaming / Studios / Global Linear Networks), Adjusted EBITDA (~$8.7B), net debt (~$29B, 3.3x), and the secular decline in linear — the target's fundamentals.

planned_sources.title: Warner Bros. Discovery investor relations / latest earnings 8-K (Ex-99)
planned_sources.rationale: Most recent quarterly results, subscriber adds, and segment commentary to update the 10-K snapshot.

planned_sources.title: Paramount Skydance earnings / results 8-K
planned_sources.rationale: Acquirer fundamentals — Paramount Skydance's own revenue, Paramount+ subscribers, and standalone leverage going into the deal (card 02 requires the acquirer, not just the target).

planned_sources.title: WBD 10-K 'Competition' + streaming market-share overview
planned_sources.rationale: Competitive positioning of HBO Max / Warner studios / networks vs. Netflix, Disney, Comcast; framing for the streaming-scale question.

planned_sources.title: U.S./global streaming subscriber & share landscape (Netflix, Disney, WBD, Paramount)
planned_sources.search_hint: 2026 streaming subscriber market share Netflix Disney Max Paramount+ comparison
planned_sources.rationale: Quantifies how sub-scale Paramount+ and HBO Max are individually vs. the leaders — needed to test the density lever in q_streaming_subscale.

planned_sources.title: Precedent media M&A: Disney-Fox (2019), Discovery-WarnerMedia (2022), AT&T-Time Warner, Comcast-Sky
planned_sources.search_hint: Disney Fox 2019 Discovery WarnerMedia 2022 AT&T Time Warner deal value premium synergies outcome
planned_sources.rationale: Precedent transactions (incl. the one that created WBD itself) for premium, synergy %, and how each fared — the comparable set for this deal's economics.

planned_sources.title: Paramount press release: 'Paramount to Acquire Warner Bros. Discovery'
planned_sources.rationale: The primary statement of deal terms, strategic rationale, and any disclosed synergy figures, in management's own words (to contrast with the independent read).

planned_sources.title: Paramount Skydance 8-K announcing the WBD merger (Ex-99.1, Feb 27 2026)
planned_sources.rationale: Primary filing of the definitive deal: $31/share, ~$110.9B value, structure, and stated rationale.

planned_sources.title: WBD board: Paramount revised proposal is a 'Company Superior Proposal'
planned_sources.rationale: The board's own articulation of why Paramount beat Netflix — directly answers q_cash_vs_stock_flip.

planned_sources.title: WBD merger proxy (DEFM14A) — 'Background of the Merger' + Opinion of Financial Advisor
planned_sources.search_hint: Warner Bros Discovery DEFM14A 2026 background of the merger opinion of financial advisor Paramount
planned_sources.rationale: The authoritative narrative of the Netflix-vs-Paramount-vs-Comcast process, the advisor's comparables/precedent/DCF multiples, and the premium math — the spine of the deal-rationale analysis.

planned_sources.title: WBD unaffected share price ($12.54, Sept 10 2025) and premium math
planned_sources.search_hint: WBD undisturbed unaffected price September 10 2025 12.54 Paramount 139% premium
planned_sources.rationale: Anchors the ~139% premium claim to the correct unaffected price (card 03 requires premium vs. unaffected, not last-traded).

planned_sources.title: Paramount Skydance 8-K — debt financing plan & leverage targets
planned_sources.rationale: Primary disclosure of the financing package size, structure, and stated post-close deleveraging plan.

planned_sources.title: Debt syndication coverage: ~$54-57.5B commitments, 6-7x leverage (Variety / PitchBook / Bloomberg)
planned_sources.rationale: Independent detail on the ~$30B high-grade / $12B high-yield / $7.5B loan structure, pro-forma debt ($79-87B), and market reception of the syndication.

planned_sources.title: Rating-agency actions on Paramount Skydance / pro-forma entity
planned_sources.search_hint: Moody's S&P Fitch Paramount Skydance Warner Bros Discovery rating downgrade leverage 2026
planned_sources.rationale: Rating actions size the cost of the leverage and any junk/investment-grade boundary risk on ~$80B+ of debt.

planned_sources.title: Paramount Skydance 8-K + Merger Agreement exhibit (Feb 27 2026)
planned_sources.rationale: Primary source for closing conditions, MAC/MAE, the $7B reverse regulatory termination fee, the $2.8B Netflix break-fee assumption, outside date (Sept 30 2026) and ticking fee — answers q_break_fee_risk_allocation.

planned_sources.title: WBD SC TO-T / tender-offer documents (Paramount tender structure)
planned_sources.rationale: The earlier hostile-tender mechanics and amendments (ticking fee origin, conditions) that became the definitive deal — context on how the structure evolved.

planned_sources.title: DOJ clears Paramount-WBD with no divestitures (June 12 2026)
planned_sources.rationale: Documents the pivotal antitrust clearance, its no-conditions nature, and the political framing (CBS+CNN under one roof) — central to q_reg_no_divestiture.

planned_sources.title: DOJ Antitrust Division approves merger — terms of clearance (Variety)
planned_sources.rationale: Second source on the DOJ rationale ('not likely to harm competition' across SVOD, linear, theatrical) to corroborate the no-remedy clearance.

planned_sources.title: FCC broadcast-license transfer vote (2-1) and dissent ('capitulation')
planned_sources.rationale: The FCC license-transfer approval and the dissent capture the second regulatory pillar and the politicization narrative feeding q_state_ag_political.

planned_sources.title: Australian ACCC clearance (waiting period to June 23 2026)
planned_sources.search_hint: ACCC Paramount Warner Bros Discovery merger authorisation June 2026
planned_sources.rationale: International clearance datapoint; the deal needs more than just US approval given WBD's global networks.

planned_sources.title: Precedent media antitrust outcomes: AT&T-Time Warner (DOJ sued, lost), Disney-Fox (RSN divestiture)
planned_sources.search_hint: AT&T Time Warner antitrust DOJ lawsuit 2018 outcome Disney Fox regional sports networks divestiture
planned_sources.rationale: The base-rate read card 04 demands — how comparable media deals cleared, were challenged, or required remedies, to judge the no-divestiture clearance and state-AG risk.

planned_sources.title: State attorneys-general threatened challenge to the merger
planned_sources.search_hint: state attorneys general lawsuit challenge Paramount Warner Bros Discovery merger June 2026 Democratic AGs
planned_sources.rationale: The principal post-DOJ litigation/close risk; who is threatening, on what theory, and whether it can enjoin the deal.

planned_sources.title: WBD 10-K 'Legal Proceedings' + deal-related shareholder litigation
planned_sources.rationale: Standing litigation on the target plus any merger-objection / disclosure suits typical of large public deals.

planned_sources.title: Senate Democrats warn FCC over foreign influence (Oracle/Ellison) in the deal
planned_sources.rationale: The national-security / foreign-influence and editorial-independence (CNN+CBS news) political overhang — a macro transmission channel into close risk and post-close controversy.

planned_sources.title: Media macro: cord-cutting pace, ad-market cyclicality, streaming economics
planned_sources.search_hint: 2026 cord cutting linear TV decline pace advertising market streaming profitability outlook
planned_sources.rationale: The exogenous demand trend that governs the value of the linear networks Paramount is levering up to buy (transmission into q_leverage_declining).

planned_sources.title: Paramount Skydance control & governance — David Ellison, board, Ellison/Oracle/RedBird backing
planned_sources.search_hint: Paramount Skydance David Ellison control structure board RedBird Oracle Larry Ellison governance
planned_sources.rationale: Acquirer control structure and key-person dynamics; who actually decides and how integration leadership is set up (q_why_now_ellison).

planned_sources.title: WBD DEF 14A proxy — executive compensation / change-of-control
planned_sources.rationale: Zaslav and WBD management change-of-control / golden-parachute terms and governance context for the board's process.

planned_sources.title: WBD DFAN14A / SC TO-T solicitation materials (Paramount contest)
planned_sources.rationale: The contested-solicitation record — the closest analogue to an activist/contest dynamic here; captures shareholder-pressure arguments during the bidding war.

planned_sources.title: Integration commentary: Paramount+ / HBO Max combination, restructuring, layoffs
planned_sources.search_hint: Paramount Warner Bros Discovery integration HBO Max Paramount+ combine layoffs restructuring plan 2026
planned_sources.rationale: Execution-risk evidence: how the two streamers and content libraries would be combined, workforce overlap, and integration commentary that a revenue/cost synergy would run through.

classes_skipped.reason: A media/entertainment combination carries no material environmental liabilities. The closest 'ESG/social' risk here — editorial independence of CNN/CBS news and foreign-influence concerns — is genuinely material and is captured under macro_geopolitical and management_governance rather than as an environmental item.

No credible dedicated short-seller (Hindenburg/Muddy Waters-style) report drives this situation; the relevant shareholder-pressure dynamics are the contested tender solicitation (DFAN14A / SC TO-T), which are captured under short_activist via the solicitation filings instead of a third-party short thesis.
